text: Which statement about firefighting foam is true?

options.text: A) To be most effective, Foam should be directed at the base of the fire
C) Foam is most effective on burning liquids which are flowing
D) Foam can only be used to extinguish Class A Fire

explanation: Firefighting foam is electrically conductive. It is most effective on Class B fires (flammable liquids) by forming a blanket that smothers the flames and prevents re-ignition, not for Class A fires.
